title: System and method for preventing unauthorized access to restricted computer systems through the use of a wireless transmitter and receiver
abstract: A method and system for automatically terminating a logout session for a restricted access system by determining that an authorized user has left the vicinity of the restricted access system. The authorized user preferably carries a wireless transmitter which transmits an authorization signal. When the authorized user leaves the vicinity of the restricted access system after logging in, the signal is no longer received by a wireless receiver or too weak of a signal, such that an access control system in communication with the wireless receiver automatically causes the restricted access system to initiate a logout action in order to prevent or reduce the chance of an unauthorized user gaining access to the restricted access system.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for automatically terminating access to a restricted access computer system which has been previously successfully logged onto by an authorized user, comprising the steps of: a. detecting by an electronic access control system in communication with a wireless receiver when the wireless receiver fails to receive an authentication signal from a wireless transmitter for the authorized user or when the authentication signal from the wireless transmitter falls below a preprogrammed authentication signal strength level, wherein the authorized user has previously successfully logged onto the restricted access computer system; b. monitoring or detecting by the electronic access control system for a length of time that the wireless receiver fails to receive an authentication signal or an authentication signal strength above the authentication signal strength threshold ievei from the wireless transmitter; and c. automatically terminating further access to the restricted access system by the electronic access control system where the length of time determined in step (b) exceeds a preprogrammed length of time. 2. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 1 further comprising the following steps prior to step a: (i) determining that the authorized has inputted one or more valid login credentials by the restricted access computer system or the access control system and (ii) receiving the authorized user's authentication signal by the access control system through initial receipt by the wireless receiver. 3. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 2 wherein the authentication signal received in step (ii) is transmitted by the wireless transmitter. 4. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 1 wherein transmission of the authentication from the wireless transmitter to the wireless receiver is through Bluetooth technology. 5. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 2 further comprising the siep, prior to step (a), of (iii) comparing identification information contained in the received authentication signal against identification information stored in an access control electronic file by the electronic access control system; and (iv) determining whether the authentication 

 signal received in step (ii) is valid by the electronic access control system based on the comparison performed in step (iii). 6. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 5 further comprising the step of denying access to the restricted access computer system prior to step (a) if one or both of the following conditions is not met: (I) valid login credentials are not inputed in step (i) or (II) a valid authorized signal is not received by the wireless receiver as determined by the access control system in step (iv). 7. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 1 wherein the step of automatically terminating access of step (c) comprises electronically sending a signal/command by the active control access system to the restricted access control system to initiate a logout 8. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 1 wherein the step of automatically terminating access of step (c) comprises electronically executing a script on the restricted access computer system to perform a logout action. 9. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 1 wherein the restricted access system is a first of at least two restricted access systems that are networked together and wherein the step of automatically terminating access of step (c) comprises electronically initiating a login event on a second restricted access system to terminate the session on the first restricted access system by way of network security protocols that only allow a single sign-in at a time on the networked computers at least two restricted access systems. 10. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 1 further comprising the steps of determining whether any key strokes have been performed at the access terminal where the authentication signal is not received or below the signal strength level but before the preprogrammed length of time has been reached. 11. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 1 further comprising the step of capturing an image or video of a person entering key strokes at an access terminal associated with the restricted access system after access to the restricted access system has been terminated in step (c). 12. The method for automatically terminating access of claim 2 wherein the one or more login credentials are selected from a group comprising: usemame, password, fingerprint scan, swipe card, badge, retinal scan or other biomeiric data. 13. A system for automatically terminating access to a restricted access computer system which has been previously successfully logged onto by an authorized user, said system comprising: an electronic access control system in communication with a restricted access computer system, said electronic access control system controlling access to the restricted access computer system and allowing access only to authorized user; an electronic access control file in communication with the electronic access control system, said electronic access control file con taining information regarding authorized users for the restricted access computer system; a wireless receiver in communication with the electronic access control system, said wireless receiver in communication with a wireless transmitter worn, carried or contained on the authorized user; wherein after said restricted access computer system has been logged onto by an authorized user said electronic access control system programmed to determine if a valid authentication signal is being received by the wireless receiver from the wireless transmitter or whether a received authentication signal is above a signal strength threshold. 14. The system for automatically terminating access of claim 13 wherein said access control system is programmed to automatically terminate a login session on the restricted access computer system where the access control system determines that a valid authentication signal is not being received from the wireless transmitter or the received authentication signal is below a signal strength threshold. 15. The system for automatically terminating access of claim 13 wherein said access control system is programmed to automatically terminate a login session on the restricted access computer system where the access control system determines that a valid authentication signal is not being received from the wireless transmitter for at least a preprogramed period of time or the received authentication signal is beiow a signal strength threshold for at least the preprogrammed period of time. 16. The system for automatically terminating access of clam 13 wherein said information includes authorization identification data for each authorized user. 17. The system for automatically terminating access of claim IS wherein for terminating a login session on the restricted access system the access control system is programmed to automatically send an electronic signal/command to the restricted access control system to initiate a logout. 18. The system for automatically terminating access of claim IS wherein for terminating a login session on the restricted access system the access control system is programmed to automatically executing a script on the restricted access computer system to perform a logout action. 19. The system for automatically terminating access of claim IS wherein the restricted access system is a first of at least two restricted access systems that are networked together and wherein for terminating a login session on the first restricted access system the access control system is programmed to automatically electronically initiate a login event on a second restricted access system to terminate the session on the first restricted access system by way of network security protocols that only allow a single sign-in at a time on the networked at least two restricted access systems. 20. The system for automatically terminating access of claim 13 further comprising a camera or video camera in communication with the access control access system, said access control system programmed to detected when key strokes are entered by a person at an access terminal associated with the restricted access system after access to the restricted access system has been terminated by said access control system and upon detection said access control system programmed to send an electronic signal to the camera or video camera to activate the camera or video camera in order to capture an image or video of the person entering the key strokes at the access terminal.
